world.name: Eridun
world.description: Eridun is the world shell that contains Mosswell, The Long Reach, and any future mapped landmasses. At first pass it is defined lightly: enough to anchor macro geography, preserve canon, and leave room for later expansion.

majorWaters.name: Greyward Expanse
majorWaters.description: A cold western sea of slate water, storms, and hard horizons. It defines the outer weather and surf character of the Reach's western coast.

majorWaters.name: Glasswake Ocean
majorWaters.description: The broader eastern ocean, brighter in weather and wider in trade possibility than the Greyward Expanse.

majorWaters.name: The Sunderdeep
majorWaters.description: A harsher southern reach of deep water and broken weather. It establishes the colder, rougher southern maritime edge of the known atlas.

landmasses.name: The Long Reach

constraints: Do not treat the reference map as a literal in-world replica.
Preserve fantasy naming and gameplay-first traversal over real-world fidelity.
Use the reference primarily to guide directional landform coherence, coastal logic, fiord structure, and inland transitions.

notes: Mosswell belongs to The Long Reach, not directly to an undifferentiated world map.
The Long Reach should be the first concrete macro-geography artifact beneath the Eridun world shell.
Later landmasses may be added without redefining Eridun.

canonRules: Eridun names the world, not a continent, kingdom, or realm subtype.
The Long Reach is the first mapped major landmass within Eridun.
Runtime prose may reflect Eridun canon and may propose candidate hints, but it must not silently redefine world structure.
Atlas seed artifacts are the authoritative machine-readable expression of world geography once they exist.
